_meta.canonical_reference: Lessiter J., Freeman J., Keogh E., Davidoff J. (2001). A cross-media presence questionnaire: The ITC-Sense of Presence Inventory. Presence: Teleoperators and Virtual Environments, 10(3), 282-297.

PHYS.name: Physical Space
PHYS.description: Sense of being physically located in the presented environment

sample_items.text: I had a sense of being in the scenes displayed.
It felt as though I was actually there in the environment.
I felt I could reach out and touch things in the environment.
My body felt like it was actually in the displayed environment.

PHYS.scoring: Sum all items (after reversing negatively-worded items), then divide by item count. Range 1-5.

ENG.name: Engagement
ENG.description: Degree of attentional and emotional involvement

sample_items.text: I was so involved in the presented world that I lost track of time.

ECO.name: Ecological Validity / Naturalness
ECO.description: How natural and consistent the environment felt with real-world expectations

sample_items.text: The way things moved was consistent with how they would move in real life.
The sounds were consistent with how they would be in real life.

NEG.name: Negative Effects
NEG.description: Discomfort, disorientation, and negative reactions during/after exposure
NEG.scoring: Mean of items. Higher scores indicate more negative effects. Range 1-5.
NEG.note: This subscale is typically analyzed separately and not included in an overall presence composite.

composite_scoring.presence_composite: Mean of PHYS, ENG, and ECO subscale means (exclude NEG). Range 1-5.

administration.timing: Administered immediately after media or VR exposure
administration.format: Paper or digital 5-point Likert; cross-media validated (TV, film, VR, desktop)
